And before that, when she was still wearing diapers, well, May didn't want her to wet her pants —A man by the naae of Christison and they had a nice plafie down there and all that, and she'd been downtown shopping one day popped I guess and went home in the afternoon and

speaker: SS
words: You know-

speaker: SS
words: (some deletions here)

speaker: VINE
words: Well, Axel's a nice man, yep, and of course, Emil, I didn't know him too good. I think there was three boys and from a nice family.

speaker: SS
words: Axel lived here for many years, didn't he?

speaker: VINE
words: Yeah. He lives in Spokane with his daughter now.

speaker: SS
words: Yeah, that's where I talked to him, in Spokane.

speaker: VINE
words: Oh, did you?

speaker: SS
words: Oh, yeah, I talked to Axel many times up there. He had a couple of wives or several- he was married several times and they all died, didn't they?

speaker: VINE
words: Yes, they did. Didn't live long. Ye;p! I didn't get to see Axel this last winter I was up there,

speaker: VINE
words: (skipping around on the tape)

speaker: SS
words: Where did you hear about that?

speaker: VINE
words: Well, let's see- it happened one afternoon and I'd been over here to that neighbor over there in the afternoon and this neighbor across the street here- coming up the street and she says, "Wasn't that awful?" And I says, "What?" "Well," she says, "there's a plane crashed up here," she says, "and killed a man.his wife and little baby." And I said, "A plane," I says, "that circled around here?" And she "Must be." And then I had a neighbor living in trailer house back here, and I came home and she knew all about it and she said, "Let's go out there." And, by that time they had every thing all cleaned up. And what I was told- now, let's see, if I can get that straight- they'd just adopted a baby and this baby wasn't very old, just a few weeks old, the way I remember- and they were going from Mount West here someplace, I can't even remember where they started from, anyway, I believe it was a rented plane they had and they were going back East to visit some of his or her folks and they were on their way and they got off from the trail or something and wound up here, and people told them not to- to be sure and not take off that way, but they did and that's how they crashed. That's the story I got. I don't know. But we found the baby's bottle and a few things like that. But that's what they said, they'd just adopted this baby.

speaker: SS
words: You know George Schmaltz told me a lot of stories about Harry Adams.

speaker: VINE
words: That's another clown, That's another- Yeah, Harry- I guess George has told that hundreds of times, and my husband, too. My husband, he went in on that with Harry- Oh, he stuck by Harry, you

speaker: VINE
words: My husband was. on that side, too- he had a lot of fun. Anyway about a lake being up in here someplace-

speaker: SS
words: Was Harry really a big liar?

speaker: VINE
words: Yes. Yeah, he could make up the best stories you ever heard! Yeah, I don't know as he just intentionally lied to hurt anybody, but just stories like that. I liked Harry awful well. He used to own this house next to me here. Yeha, th*y had a lot of fun with that salesman. Go audi

speaker: SS
words: Are those stories that George made famous or were they stories that were well known when they happened? Do you think they were wellknoxvn when Harry told them?

speaker: VINE
words: Yeah.

speaker: SS
words: Lot of guys knew 'em?

speaker: VINE
words: Yeah. I think so. I don't know. Harry might have made it up right there and then and %. might have helped him. He was good at that, too. Oh, boy, yeah, they could make it sound real good.

speaker: SS
words: Have the huckleberries always been good around town here?

speaker: VINE
words: Well, most generally. Last year there wasn't very many nor the year before, but this year they should really be good again if the frost didn't kill them. But we had a^frost here not long ago, so I don't know. I haven't had a chance to go out, I'm been dying to go- get up in the edge of the woods someplace and see.

speaker: SS
words: Isn't it still a little early?

speaker: VINE
words: Yes, it is . Way up high it'll be the latter part of August before they get ripe. I want to pick quite a few, I have a granddaughter over in Washington. She'd rather have huckleberries than anything in the world, and she'll just open up a jar of 'em without any sugar or anything and set and eat a quart of 'em. And why not have them when there's plenty of em? Maybe I'd better go and get the recipe. I believe it's four cups of sugar, two lemons, a quart of cow's milk, and a can of condensed milk and a quart of huckleberries and . And it's the best darned stuff you've ever eaten.

speaker: SS
words: You just freeze it? You just mix it together and freeze it?

speaker: VINE
words: Yeah.

speaker: SS
words: You don't have to-

speaker: VINE
words: Yes, your freezer would be the best- electric freezer or hand turned

speaker: SS
words: That sounds good.

speaker: VINE
words: It is good. I made huckleberry pies for Fourth of July. I got down in the basement and I thought, well, I'll just fool 'em one day- they'll think I've been out in the hills agathering 'em. I made a couple of huckleberry pies-

speaker: VINE
words: END SIDE E
